title: python-multipart 0.0.29 vulnerable to DoS and parameter smuggling (4 CVEs)
description: Superset pins python-multipart==0.0.29 (requirements/base.txt), which is affected by four advisories: CVE-2026-53540 (negative Content-Length buffers the entire request body in memory -> memory-exhaustion DoS), CVE-2026-53539 and CVE-2026-53538 (semicolon separator handling enables querystring parameter smuggling), and CVE-2026-53537 (RFC 2231/5987 Content-Disposition parameter smuggling). All are resolved by upgrading to 0.0.31. This library parses multipart/form-data on Superset's upload endpoints, so this is directly reachable.

title: jaraco.context 6.0.1 path traversal (CVE-2026-23949)
description: Superset pins jaraco.context==6.0.1, which contains a path traversal vulnerability in its archive/tarball extraction helper (CVE-2026-23949). An attacker-controlled archive can write files outside the intended extraction directory. Fixed in 6.1.0. This is a minor version bump with no expected breaking changes.

title: urijs 1.19.8 open redirect / protocol confusion (3 CVEs)
description: The Superset frontend (superset-frontend) depends on urijs==1.19.8, affected by CVE-2022-1243 and CVE-2022-1233 (incorrect protocol extraction via control characters) and CVE-2022-0868 (open redirect). These enable URL-confusion and open-redirect attacks. All are fixed by upgrading to 1.19.11. Update package.json and the lockfile so the resolved version is consistent.

title: pytest 7.4.4 insecure tmpdir handling (CVE-2025-71176)
description: The Superset development requirements pin pytest==7.4.4, which has insecure temporary-directory handling (CVE-2025-71176) allowing a local attacker to pre-create predictable tmp paths. This is a dev/test-only dependency; upgrading to a fixed 8.x/9.x line may require minor test-collection adjustments, so validate the test suite still collects and runs.

title: Flask 2.3.3 missing Vary: Cookie header can leak cached session data (CVE-2026-27205)
description: Superset pins Flask==2.3.3. Under certain access patterns Flask fails to emit a `Vary: Cookie` response header, which can cause a shared/CDN cache to serve one user's session-dependent response to another. Fixed in the 3.1.x line. NOTE: moving from Flask 2.x to 3.x is a major upgrade and may cascade into Flask-AppBuilder / Werkzeug breaking changes across Superset. This is intentionally included as a hard case: remediate only if it is safely achievable, otherwise report it as partial/failed with the blocker.
